rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 16, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors¿ (on behalf of their insured, defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.) motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability, and survival) and granted as to issue 4 (punitive damages). As to summary judgment and issues 1, 2 and 3 of summary adjudication, Ms. Sultz¿s depositon testimony, Rich-Tex, Inc.¿s responses to general order 129 interrogatories and Mr. Watkins¿ testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Montgomery was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (joint compound) attributable to Rich-Tex. As to whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Montgomery¿s risk of developing mesothelioma, Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Intervenors did not satisfy their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Montgomery¿s exposure to asbestos-containing Rich-Tex joint compound was a substantial factor. Intervenors¿ failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim that exposure attributable to Rich-Tex was a substantial factor precludes Intervenors from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Intervenors also did not satisfy their burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims against them. Mr. Cohen¿s declaration fails to take into account much of Ms. Sultz¿s deposition testimony regarding Mr. Montgomery¿s work with Rich-Tex joint compound and is thus insufficient to allow defendant to sustain its burden. As to adjudication issue 4 (punitive damages), Intervenors sustained their initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether Rich-Tex acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Intervenors¿ objections to Ms. Sultz¿s deposition testimony are overruled. The court considered plaintiffs¿ opposition. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DELMAR STEPHENS VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sears, Roebuck And Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, OCTOBER 16, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Sears, Roebuck and Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden as it failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Macarus¿ declaration are sustained. Defendant did not attempt to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages which was the only issue defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DELMAR STEPHENS VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kaiser Ventures Llc¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Or Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, OCTOBER 16, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Kaiser Ventures LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 1 (Workers¿ Compensation) and denied as to issues 2 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability) and 3 (strict liability). As to issue 1, plaintiff represents that he does not contend that defendant is liable for injuries arising out of his employment by defendant thus this issue is unopposed. As to issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his negligence and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action. Defendant¿s failure to attach to its moving papers plaintiff¿s factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support these claims precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of either of these causes of action. As to issue 3, defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his strict liability cause of action. Defendant¿s failure to attach to its moving papers plaintiff¿s factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support this cause of action precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of either of this cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
